items.title: The NBA's waiting game: LeBron James and the biggest remaining questions of the offseason
items.summary: Welcome to the dark days of the offseason. Summer League was a nice solvent for those of us starved of legitimate basketball, but that's come and gone. There are still a number of items that need to be addressed this offseason, ones that are holding up the rest of the league in one way or another. Let's take a look.What's the holdup with LeBron? If the mere visualization of LeBron James' latest free agency saga wasn't the biggest indicator that the NBA offseason has reached its Rubicon point, I'm not sure how else to explain it. These are dark times. This is perhaps the first time in league history where 98% of the basketball world has been in the dark for this long about where a player will eventually end up. Let's even push that to 99%. Social media is clinging to every word and every episode of the Game Over podcast with James' agent Rich…

items.title: Where will LeBron James play next season? Next team odds, predictions, best fits including Cavaliers, Warriors
items.summary: It's been more than three weeks since LeBron James formally told the Los Angeles Lakers he was not returning, and while he said from the start he would take his time with this decision, how long this has dragged out has become the story.Where do things stand with LeBron's decision? Where is LeBron going and when will he make his announcement? Here is all the latest information.Where will LeBron James play next season?Only one person knows that, and he's not talking. Yet.This is LeBron's call, and his friend and agent Rich Paul has said publicly — echoing what some teams in the mix have said privately — that even he doesn't really know where LeBron is going. Or when the decision is coming. This is all on LeBron, and he had his circle keep things tight and don't leak info. Even his son and teammate in Los Angeles, Bronny James, doesn't know."You think your…

items.title: Celtics, Jordan Walsh reportedly agree to 3-year contract extension
items.summary: Jordan Walsh has agreed to a three-year, $16 million contract extension with the Boston Celtics, according to NBA TV's Chris Haynes.As part of the agreement, the Celtics will pick up Walsh's $2.4 million team option for the 2026-27 NBA season before adding three more years onto the deal, which will run through 2029-30, according to ESPN's Shams Charania. Boston will hold a team option for that '29-30 season, according to Mike Scotto of HoopsHype.Including the exercised team option, the deal locks in an estimated $12.7 million in guaranteed money over the next three years for the 22-year-old Walsh, whom the Celtics drafted out of Arkansas in the second round of the 2023 NBA Draft, and who has made just over $5.2 million across the first three seasons of his career.For Boston, it also locks in a rising rotation-caliber wing at what will amount to about 3% of the salary cap…

items.title: Rockets reportedly hiring shooting specialist Chip Engelland in move that could help Houston's young core
items.summary: The Houston Rockets are hiring longtime NBA assistant Chip Engelland, one of the league's most respected and decorated shooting specialists, to join Ime Udoka's coaching staff, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.Engelland, 65, played at Duke in the late 1970s and early 1980s before plying his trade in the Philippines and in the now-defunct Continental Basketball Association for several years. A knockdown shooter at the collegiate and pro levels, he entered the NBA's coaching ranks in 1999 as a shooting consultant for the Detroit Pistons under Alvin Gentry, beginning his career working with young superstar Grant Hill. In Hill's first five pro seasons, he went a combined 22-for-121, or 18.2%, from 3-point range. The season Engelland got to Detroit, he shot 34-for-98, 34.7%, from deep. After that season, Hill signed with the Orlando Magic in free agency; he wouldn't…

items.title: A Reason For The Rangers To Be Patient For Now
items.summary: Danny Wild-Imagn ImagesThere are many ways to use the Knicks' success story to the Blueshirts advantage.  Ticketholder Mark Linde writes from his New Jersey estate with insightful ideas."The big lesson for the Rangers from the 'Down The Hall' Knicks is NOT TO RUSH THINGS. Here's the genuine reason why:"A few years ago the Knicks had a good nucleus of young players. But instead of building on that growing foundation, they blew it upon a trade for an aging star Carmelo Anthony. TheRangers now have a nice, young, promising group."That tells me that management should stay the course and build around it – even if it takes a few more years than you like.-"The obvious, larger question is whether or not Chris Drury is the right man to remodel this franchise and whether Mike Sullivan is the coach to mold a new

items.title: Defensive wing Jordan Walsh reportedly extends with Boston on three-year, $16 million deal
items.summary: Jordan Walsh already was going to be back in Boston next season, the team had picked up his $2.4 million option, but now he is going to be a fixture on the wing there for years.Walsh and the Celtics agreed to a three-year, $16 million extension, something first reported by NBA insider Chris Haynes and since confirmed by other reports. The first two years of this deal are fully guaranteed, with the third being a team option. This locks Walsh in with Boston for at least three more years at a very affordable price for a rotation player.Walsh played in 68 games last season for the Celtics, averaging 5.4 points and four rebounds a game, and his 3-point shot has improved, with him knocking down 38.4% from beyond the arc last season. However, his defense was really his calling card. He is a high-level on-ball defender on the wing, which is where Joe Mazzulla leaned into him.LOOK AT THE DEFENSE…

items.title: 8 backup plans for the Sixers if they whiff on LeBron James
items.summary: MADRID, SPAIN - JUNE 02: Mario Hezonja of Real Madrid gestures after losing the Spanish basketball League Liga ACB Endesa Quarter Finals Game 1 between Real Madrid and La Laguna Tenerife at Movistar Arena on June 02, 2026, in Madrid, Spain. (Photo By Irina R. Hipolito/Europa Press via Getty Images) | Europa Press via Getty Images The Sixers have one open roster spot at the moment, and it’s fairly obvious whom they’re saving it for. But if LeBron James chooses to sign elsewhere in free agency, then what?The free-agent market has largely been picked over at this point. A handful of big names are still unsigned, although James Harden and Draymond Green are just biding their time until LeBron decides before they re-sign with the Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors, respectively. Neither are legitimate possibilities for the Sixers.The Sixers already split their non-taxpayer…
